zh.lifeSummary: 當Beatrice Stafford於大約1341年出生在英國斯塔福爾時，她的父親Sir Ralph Stafford41歲，母親Margaret de Audley32歲。她於大約1350年與Maurice Fitzgerald 2nd Earl of Desmond結婚。她去世於1415年4月14日在英國英格蘭諾斯 越可爾爾姆斯利享年75歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭越可爾 易亞斯特 利丁瓦特。

nameMeanings.name: Stafford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name principally from Stafford (Staffordshire), but occasionally from other places with similar names, such as Stafford House in Ifield (Sussex), possibly East and West Stowford in East Down (Devon), and three minor places in Devon called Stafford, in Dolton, Broadhembury, and Colyton parishes. The places are all named with Old English ford ‘ford’ as the final element, but have different initial elements. The Staffordshire placename has Old English stæth ‘river bank, shore’; Stafford in Colyton (Devon) has Old English stān ‘stone, rock’; the Sussex placename has Old English stēor ‘steer, bullock’; East and West Stowford (Devon) have Old English stæf ‘staff, stave, rod’; Stafford in Dolton and Stafford in Broadhembury (Devon) may have Old English stæth, stān, or stæf.

variants.name: Stocker
meanings: German and Swiss German (also Stöcker): topographic name for someone living by a tree stump (see Stock 3) or an occupational name for a tree cutter, from Middle High German stocken ‘to clear land’.
German and Swiss German (also Stöcker); Dutch: occupational name from Middle High German, Middle Dutch stocker ‘jailer’.

English: occupational name from Middle English stokker ‘one who sells stockfish’ (fish dried in the air without salt). This was the usual source of the name in medieval London, where a bylaw of 1419 stated that no stokker should board a ship to buy fish (presumably in order to forestall the market).

nameMeanings.name: Beatrice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: and French form of   Beatrix  , which was quite popular in England during the Middle Ages, and strongly revived in the 19th century. It is most famous as the name of Dante's beloved, and is borne by the elder (  b. 1988 ) of the Duke of York's daughters.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Beatrice Stafford was born about 1341, in Staffordshire, England, her father, Sir Ralph Stafford, was 41 and her mother, Margaret de Audley, was 32. She married Maurice Fitzgerald 2nd Earl of Desmond about 1350. She died on 14 April 1415, in Helmsley, North Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 75, and was buried in Warter, Yorkshire East Riding, England, United Kingdom.

Elle a épousé Maurice Fitzgerald 2nd Earl of Desmond environ 1350. Elle est décédée le 14 avril 1415, à Helmsley, North Yorkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 75 ans, et a été enterrée à Warter, Yorkshire East Riding, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.lifeSummary: Beatrice Stafford(이)가 대략 1341년 영국 스태퍼드실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Ralph Stafford(은)는 41세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret de Audley(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 대략 1350년 Maurice Fitzgerald 2nd Earl of Desmond(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1415년 4월 14일 영국 잉글랜드 노르스 요르크실르 헬슬리에서 75세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 이스트 리딩 워터에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Maurice Fitzgerald 2nd Earl of Desmond aproximadamente 1350. ru.Warter, Yorkshire, England: вартир, еркшир эаст ридинг, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Beatrice Stafford родилась около 1341 г. в местности стаффордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Ralph Stafford было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Margaret de Audley, 32. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Maurice Fitzgerald 2nd Earl of Desmond около 1350 г. Она умерла 14 апреля 1415 г. в местности хелмсли, норт еркшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 75 лет и была похоронена в местности вартир, еркшир эаст ридинг, Англия, Великобритания.
